message:
fix(ft-nn): HingeEmbeddingLoss composes through tensor primitives [bd-8530]

HingeEmbeddingLoss::forward_hinge used to extract input + target
values, compute the per-element loss in plain f64, and rebuild
a requires_grad=false leaf. The subsequent tensor_mean preserved
autograd from the leaf onward but the leaf had already severed
the tape — input never received any gradient.

Compose through autograd-aware primitives:
  mask = (target > 0) ? 1.0 : 0.0   [non-grad, target is labels]
  pos_branch = mask * input
  neg_branch = (1 - mask) * relu(margin - input)
  loss = mean(pos_branch + neg_branch)

Same per-element math as before, but the autograd tape is
preserved through input.

First child of frankentorch-cq0b (ft-nn loss-severance umbrella).
Closes frankentorch-8530. ft-nn lib tests: 517 passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -49,6 +49,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-7yvv","title":"functional_dropout severs autograd; mask is supposed to flow gradient through input","description":"functional_dropout at lib.rs:5199 generates random mask values, multiplies in plain f64, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf — silently severing autograd through dropout. Every transformer / convnet training that uses dropout would have gradients of input dropped to zero by the dropout layer's masking. PyTorch's torch.nn.functional.dropout is differentiable: the mask is sampled per-forward (non-differentiable random) but the multiplication input * mask * scale carries gradient through input.

Fix: replace the manual values+leaf assembly with composition through autograd-aware tensor_mul:
   mask_vals = 0 where dropped, scale=1/(1-p) where surviving (random per-element)
   mask = tensor_variable(mask_vals, shape, false)   # non-grad mask is correct
   output = tensor_mul(input, mask)                  # autograd-aware

Same observable forward behavior, but the autograd tape is preserved for the input.

Critical severity — this affects every dropout-using training run. Sister of the recent loss-severance cluster (ilwr / 4liu / tr2a / x9cr). Surfaced during /reality-check pass.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Replaced functional_dropout's manual mask-and-scale body with composition through tensor_mul against a non-grad random mask. The mask values are 0 (dropped) or scale = 1/(1-p) (kept), generated per-element via the RNG; multiplication preserves the autograd tape so input gradients flow as expected. Same observable forward behavior, but training paths through dropout layers now actually train.

Critical-severity fix: every dropout-using transformer / convnet training was previously getting zero gradient downstream of any dropout layer, making training a no-op for the affected sub-network.

Added functional_dropout_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (n=100, p=0.5 → grad entries are 0 or 1/(1-p) = 2 element-wise; sanity check that at least 10 survive). ft-api lib tests: 1155.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:28:27.245880709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:30:28.339787711Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:30:28.336707656Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-80m","title":"Add bernoulli and poisson random sampling ops","description":"Implement tensor_randint, tensor_randperm, tensor_bernoulli, tensor_multinomial in ft-api. These are commonly used PyTorch tensor creation operations needed for drop-in parity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T20:35:11.039100137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T20:45:35.039477572Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T20:45:35.039162396Z","close_reason":"Implemented bernoulli, bernoulli_p, and poisson ops in ft-api with 8 tests passing. randint, randperm, multinomial already existed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-82b","title":"Add distributed training support (DDP/FSDP equivalents)","description":"FrankenTorch currently has no distributed-process orchestration, gradient synchronization, sharded parameter management, collective communication surface, or optimizer/state partitioning comparable to PyTorch DDP/FSDP.

Scope for eventual closure:
1. Define distributed process-group / collective abstractions
2. Add replicated gradient synchronization for DDP-style training
3. Add parameter/state sharding for FSDP-style execution
4. Preserve deterministic replay and audit evidence across multi-rank execution
5. Add conformance scenarios for cross-rank gradient equivalence and failure handling

This bead exists so FEATURE_PARITY.md can explicitly mark distributed training as deferred rather than implying it is already covered by the current CPU eager-mode slice.","status":"deferred","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:23:55.689135222Z","created_by":"CopperStork","updated_at":"2026-04-28T03:34:51.412246490Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-8530","title":"Fix HingeEmbeddingLoss autograd via tensor_where + relu [child of cq0b]","description":"HingeEmbeddingLoss::forward_hinge at lib.rs:11003 computes elementwise:
   loss_i = x_i        if y_i > 0
          = max(0, margin - x_i)  otherwise
Composable: y_pos_mask = (y > 0), then mask * x + (1 - mask) * relu(margin - x). Or tensor_where over the same condition.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Composed HingeEmbeddingLoss through tensor_relu + mask-based blend (mask is non-grad target-derived; pos_branch = mask*input; neg_branch = (1-mask)*relu(margin-input); loss = mean(pos+neg)). All ops autograd-aware. ft-nn lib tests: 517.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T23:14:52.527960320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T23:16:02.892148804Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T23:16:02.889800397Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-88p","title":"tensor_nonzero on 0-d scalar always returns shape [0, 0]","description":"FrankenTorchSession::tensor_nonzero() at crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs:10260 silently returns shape [0, 0] for any 0-d (scalar) input — including non-zero scalars where PyTorch returns shape [1, 0].

Root cause at line 10309:
    let num_nonzero = indices.len().checked_div(ndim).unwrap_or(0);
When ndim == 0 (scalar), the inner stride loop runs 0 times so 'indices' stays empty for any value, and 0.checked_div(0).unwrap_or(0) = 0 — losing the 'is non-zero' signal entirely.

PyTorch parity:
- torch.nonzero(torch.tensor(5.0)) -> shape [1, 0]
- torch.nonzero(torch.tensor(0.0)) -> shape [0, 0]
- torch.nonzero(torch.tensor(float('nan'))) -> shape [1, 0]  (NaN treated as nonzero)

Fix: track non-zero count explicitly instead of inferring it from indices.len() / ndim, and also clean up the related tensor_nonzero_as_tuple which currently returns an empty Vec for any scalar (matches PyTorch '() tuple' but worth documenting).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:29:42.285819874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:40:16.660618429Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T20:40:16.658857234Z","close_reason":"tensor_nonzero now tracks num_nonzero explicitly so 0-d scalars produce the correct PyTorch-equivalent shape: non-zero scalar -> [1, 0], zero scalar -> [0, 0], NaN scalar -> [1, 0] (NaN treated as nonzero). Previously indices.len().checked_div(0).unwrap_or(0) collapsed all scalars to [0, 0]. 3 new scalar tests added; all 1082 ft-api tests pass; clippy + ubs clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-89t","title":"Add tensor_select, tensor_take_along_dim, tensor_scatter_reduce","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:14:13.280870191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:24:04.979536012Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:24:04.979258527Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_select, tensor_take_along_dim, tensor_scatter_reduce with 8 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-8e7","title":"Harden ft-serialize decode paths against panic-prone byte conversions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:08:20.502180967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:11:54.220601650Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:11:54.220341307Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-c84","title":"Diagnose residual beads integrity-check warning","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:27:54.222375196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:35:07.976091802Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:35:07.975908695Z","close_reason":"Diagnosed residual integrity warning: live DB and JSONL stay in sync; isolated rebuild reproduces the warning; VACUUM yields a clean DB; subsequent normal br write paths reintroduce 'never used' pages, pointing to a beads_rust/frankensqlite writer/page-accounting bug rather than JSONL drift.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-cbyx","title":"MaxUnpool1d/2d/3d sever autograd; scatter-then-gather backward needed","description":"MaxUnpool1d (line 6149), MaxUnpool2d (line 6237), and MaxUnpool3d (line 6337) all extract input values, scatter them via indices in plain f64, and rebuild requires_grad=false leaves. PyTorch's torch.nn.MaxUnpool* IS differentiable.

Backward: each output position came from a single input position (via the indices) — gather grad_output from those positions back to grad_input. Sister of repeat_interleave / diag_embed / unfold (gather-scatter pattern, except here forward is scatter and backward is gather, so the mapping inverts).

Each variant has the same forward/backward structure with different spatial indexing.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. All three MaxUnpool variants (1d/2d/3d) now wrap their forward in tensor_apply_function with the analytical scatter-then-gather backward. The (input_pos → output_pos) indices mapping is captured by both forward and backward closures; backward gathers grad_output from the destination positions back to grad_input. ft-nn lib tests: 517.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T23:09:32.514618605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T23:12:12.984821113Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T23:12:12.981744788Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-cg0","title":"Align detailed crate contract table with current FrankenTorch crate names","description":"Update section 14 of COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md so the crate contract table uses ft-core, ft-kernel-cpu, ft-api, ft-runtime, ft-device, and ft-data instead of the older ft-types/ft-tensor/ft-ops/frankentorch naming.","notes":"Updated section 14 of the comprehensive spec so the detailed crate contract table now uses the actual workspace crates: ft-core, ft-device, ft-kernel-cpu, ft-api, ft-data, ft-runtime, plus the existing ft-dispatch/ft-autograd/ft-nn/ft-optim/ft-serialize/ft-conformance entries.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:18:43.486151087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:19:12.519560308Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:19:12.518714757Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-cq0b","title":"ft-nn loss modules sever autograd: HingeEmbeddingLoss, FocalLoss, MultiMarginLoss, others","description":"Multiple loss modules in ft-nn extract values, compute the per-element loss in plain f64, and rebuild a non-grad leaf via tensor_variable(_, _, false). The subsequent reduction (mean/sum/none) operates on this severed leaf so gradients never reach the input.

Affected (line numbers from current main):
  - HingeEmbeddingLoss::forward_hinge (lib.rs:11003)
  - FocalLoss / its forward (lib.rs:~11800, near 11810)
  - MultiMarginLoss (lib.rs:~11827)
  - Other reduction-style loss modules in the 11800-12100 range

PyTorch's torch.nn.HingeEmbeddingLoss / FocalLoss / MultiMarginLoss are all differentiable. Each loss has a closed-form composition through autograd-aware primitives.

Children: each loss gets its own fix bead. Sister of frankentorch-2med (the session-method loss umbrella that closed earlier).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T23:14:43.037077362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T23:14:43.037077362Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-cwl","title":"Add conformance coverage for torch.nn.init parity surface","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T19:47:37.193879144Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T19:57:11.433715035Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T19:57:11.433447830Z","close_reason":"Added dedicated tensor_init conformance fixture family and smoke coverage; verified targeted ft-conformance test/check via rch; remaining clippy failure is unrelated pre-existing ft-api lint drift","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-d180","title":"tensor_diag_embed is severed-autograd: linear backward, easy fix","description":"tensor_diag_embed at lib.rs:3444 places a 1-D input vector onto the diagonal (or sub/super-diagonal) of a zero-initialized 2-D matrix. PyTorch's torch.diag_embed is differentiable. The forward is purely linear in the input:
   output[row, col] = input[i] if (row, col) is on the requested diagonal else 0
so the backward is just the diagonal-extraction:
   grad_input[i] = grad_output[row, col]
where (row, col) is the same position the forward placed input[i] in.

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with this analytical backward. Save offset and k for backward; or capture via closure. Surfaced during /reality-check pass scanning for the values-then-non-grad-leaf pattern.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_diag_embed in tensor_apply_function with diagonal-extraction backward: grad_input[i] = grad_output[row, col] for the same (row, col) the forward placed input[i]. Captured offset (positive vs negative) and abs_offset via closure (Copy-by-move). Added diag_embed_propagates_gradient_via_diagonal_extraction regression test using a weighted sum over a 3x3 main-diagonal embedding (input [1,2,3] gets gradient [1,5,9] = weights[i,i]). ft-api lib tests: 1145.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T18:18:09.193738126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:20:07.536166362Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T18:20:07.533806900Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-d214","title":"tensor_combinations severs autograd; gather-then-scatter backward","description":"tensor_combinations at lib.rs:4169 enumerates r-element combinations from a 1-D input (with or without replacement). Each output cell is a copy of one input element via a combinatorial mapping. PyTorch's torch.combinations IS differentiable.

Same pattern as cartesian_prod / unfold: pre-compute the (output_pos → input_pos) mapping during the recursive combo enumeration, then wrap in tensor_apply_function with gather-scatter backward.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_combinations in tensor_apply_function with gather-then-scatter backward. Pre-computed (output_pos → input_pos) mapping during the recursive combo enumeration (using indices instead of values), captured in both forward and backward closures. Added combinations_propagates_gradient_via_scatter regression test (input=[10,20,30], r=2 no-replacement → 3 combos, each input appears in 2 rows → grad=[2,2,2]). ft-api lib tests: 1168.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T22:27:17.668967639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T22:29:36.243252154Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T22:29:36.240904950Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
